     686                 :             : 
     687                 :             :     /**
     688                 :             :      * DAB modulation scheme. SPS (Single Phase Shift): only D3 is non-zero. EPS (Extended Phase
     689                 :             :      * Shift): D1>0 on primary bridge, D2=0, D3 controls power. DPS (Dual Phase Shift): D1=D2>0
     690                 :             :      * symmetric, D3 controls power. TPS (Triple Phase Shift): D1, D2, D3 independent. Ref:
     691                 :             :      * Huang et al., Energies 11(9):2419 (2018).
     692                 :             :      */
     693                 :             :     enum class ModulationType : int { DPS, EPS, SPS, TPS };
     694                 :             : 
     695                 :             :     /**
     696                 :             :      * One DAB operating point. Modulation is parameterised by three phase-shift variables D1,
     697                 :             :      * D2, D3 (Huang et al. 2018 convention): D1 and D2 are intra-bridge (leg-to-leg) shifts
     698                 :             :      * that control each bridge's effective duty cycle, and D3 is the outer inter-bridge shift
     699                 :             :      * that controls power transfer. SPS uses D3 only (D1=D2=0). EPS uses D3 + one inner shift
     700                 :             :      * (D1, with D2=0). DPS uses D3 + one symmetric inner shift (D1=D2). TPS uses all three
     701                 :             :      * independently.
     702                 :             :      */
     703                 :             :     class DabOperatingPoint {
     704                 :             :         public:
     705                 :           0 :         DabOperatingPoint() = default;
     706                 :           0 :         virtual ~DabOperatingPoint() = default;
     707                 :             : 
     708                 :             :         private:
     709                 :             :         double ambient_temperature;
     710                 :             :         std::optional<double> inner_phase_shift1;
     711                 :             :         std::optional<double> inner_phase_shift2;
     712                 :             :         std::optional<double> inner_phase_shift3;
     713                 :             :         std::optional<ModulationType> modulation_type;
     714                 :             :         std::vector<double> output_currents;
     715                 :             :         std::vector<double> output_voltages;
     716                 :             :         double switching_frequency;
     717                 :             : 
     718                 :             :         public:
     719                 :             :         /**
     720                 :             :          * The ambient temperature of the operating point
     721                 :             :          */
     722                 :           0 :         const double & get_ambient_temperature() const { return ambient_temperature; }
     723                 :             :         double & get_mutable_ambient_temperature() { return ambient_temperature; }
     724                 :           0 :         void set_ambient_temperature(const double & value) { this->ambient_temperature = value; }
     725                 :             : 
     726                 :             :         /**
     727                 :             :          * D1 — primary-bridge intra-leg phase shift in degrees (shift between the two legs of the
     728                 :             :          * primary full-bridge). Range [0, 90). 0 = square wave (50% duty). Used in EPS, DPS, TPS.
     729                 :             :          */
     730                 :           0 :         std::optional<double> get_inner_phase_shift1() const { return inner_phase_shift1; }
     731                 :           0 :         void set_inner_phase_shift1(std::optional<double> value) { this->inner_phase_shift1 = value; }
     732                 :             : 
     733                 :             :         /**
     734                 :             :          * D2 — secondary-bridge intra-leg phase shift in degrees (shift between the two legs of the
     735                 :             :          * secondary full-bridge). Range [0, 90). 0 = square wave (50% duty). Used in TPS. For DPS,
     736                 :             :          * if absent, defaults to D1 (symmetric).
     737                 :             :          */
     738                 :           0 :         std::optional<double> get_inner_phase_shift2() const { return inner_phase_shift2; }
     739                 :           0 :         void set_inner_phase_shift2(std::optional<double> value) { this->inner_phase_shift2 = value; }
     740                 :             : 
     741                 :             :         /**
     742                 :             :          * D3 — inter-bridge (outer) phase shift in degrees between primary and secondary bridges.
     743                 :             :          * Signed: positive = forward power flow (primary → secondary). Used in all modulation
     744                 :             :          * modes. This is the primary power-transfer control variable.
     745                 :             :          */
     746                 :           0 :         std::optional<double> get_inner_phase_shift3() const { return inner_phase_shift3; }
     747                 :           0 :         void set_inner_phase_shift3(std::optional<double> value) { this->inner_phase_shift3 = value; }
     748                 :             : 
     749                 :             :         /**
     750                 :             :          * DAB modulation scheme. SPS (Single Phase Shift): only D3 is non-zero. EPS (Extended Phase
     751                 :             :          * Shift): D1>0 on primary bridge, D2=0, D3 controls power. DPS (Dual Phase Shift): D1=D2>0
     752                 :             :          * symmetric, D3 controls power. TPS (Triple Phase Shift): D1, D2, D3 independent. Ref:
     753                 :             :          * Huang et al., Energies 11(9):2419 (2018).
     754                 :             :          */
     755                 :           0 :         std::optional<ModulationType> get_modulation_type() const { return modulation_type; }
     756                 :           0 :         void set_modulation_type(std::optional<ModulationType> value) { this->modulation_type = value; }
     757                 :             : 
     758                 :             :         /**
     759                 :             :          * A list of output currents, one per output
     760                 :             :          */
     761                 :           0 :         const std::vector<double> & get_output_currents() const { return output_currents; }
     762                 :             :         std::vector<double> & get_mutable_output_currents() { return output_currents; }
     763                 :           0 :         void set_output_currents(const std::vector<double> & value) { this->output_currents = value; }
     764                 :             : 
     765                 :             :         /**
     766                 :             :          * A list of output voltages, one per output
     767                 :             :          */
     768                 :           0 :         const std::vector<double> & get_output_voltages() const { return output_voltages; }
     769                 :             :         std::vector<double> & get_mutable_output_voltages() { return output_voltages; }
     770                 :           0 :         void set_output_voltages(const std::vector<double> & value) { this->output_voltages = value; }
     771                 :             : 
     772                 :             :         /**
     773                 :             :          * The switching frequency of the operating point
     774                 :             :          */
     775                 :           0 :         const double & get_switching_frequency() const { return switching_frequency; }
     776                 :             :         double & get_mutable_switching_frequency() { return switching_frequency; }
     777                 :           0 :         void set_switching_frequency(const double & value) { this->switching_frequency = value; }
     778                 :             :     };
     779                 :             : 
     780                 :             :     /**
     781                 :             :      * Dual Active Bridge (DAB) DC-DC converter excitation. Modulation parameters follow the
     782                 :             :      * standard Triple Phase Shift (TPS) convention: D1 = primary-bridge intra-leg shift, D2 =
     783                 :             :      * secondary-bridge intra-leg shift, D3 = inter-bridge (outer) shift. See Huang et al.,
     784                 :             :      * Energies 11(9):2419 (2018) and Maniktala & Rosano, 'Power Supplies A to Z', 3rd ed. (Ch.
     785                 :             :      * 2).
     786                 :             :      */
     787                 :             :     class DualActiveBridge {
     788                 :             :         public:
     789                 :           0 :         DualActiveBridge() = default;
     790                 :           0 :         virtual ~DualActiveBridge() = default;
     791                 :             : 
     792                 :             :         private:
     793                 :             :         std::optional<double> efficiency;
     794                 :             :         DimensionWithTolerance input_voltage;
     795                 :             :         std::vector<DabOperatingPoint> operating_points;
     796                 :             :         std::optional<std::vector<double>> per_secondary_leakage;
     797                 :             :         std::optional<double> series_inductance;
     798                 :             :         std::optional<bool> use_leakage_inductance;
     799                 :             : 
     800                 :             :         public:
     801                 :             :         /**
     802                 :             :          * The target efficiency
     803                 :             :          */
     804                 :             :         std::optional<double> get_efficiency() const { return efficiency; }
     805                 :           0 :         void set_efficiency(std::optional<double> value) { this->efficiency = value; }
     806                 :             : 
     807                 :             :         /**
     808                 :             :          * The input voltage of the DAB converter
     809                 :             :          */
     810                 :           0 :         const DimensionWithTolerance & get_input_voltage() const { return input_voltage; }
     811                 :             :         DimensionWithTolerance & get_mutable_input_voltage() { return input_voltage; }
     812                 :           0 :         void set_input_voltage(const DimensionWithTolerance & value) { this->input_voltage = value; }
     813                 :             : 
     814                 :             :         /**
     815                 :             :          * A list of operating points
     816                 :             :          */
     817                 :           0 :         const std::vector<DabOperatingPoint> & get_operating_points() const { return operating_points; }
     818                 :             :         std::vector<DabOperatingPoint> & get_mutable_operating_points() { return operating_points; }
     819                 :           0 :         void set_operating_points(const std::vector<DabOperatingPoint> & value) { this->operating_points = value; }
     820                 :             : 
     821                 :             :         /**
     822                 :             :          * Optional per-secondary leakage inductance in H, one entry per output. Required for
     823                 :             :          * physically meaningful multi-output DAB simulation; if absent, all secondaries share the
     824                 :             :          * same series inductance.
     825                 :             :          */
     826                 :             :         std::optional<std::vector<double>> get_per_secondary_leakage() const { return per_secondary_leakage; }
     827                 :           0 :         void set_per_secondary_leakage(std::optional<std::vector<double>> value) { this->per_secondary_leakage = value; }
     828                 :             : 
     829                 :             :         /**
     830                 :             :          * The series inductance for power transfer control. If 0, uses transformer leakage
     831                 :             :          * inductance
     832                 :             :          */

    1919                 :             :     /**

    1923                 :             :     enum class SubApplication : int { COMMON_MODE_NOISE_FILTERING, DIFFERENTIAL_MODE_NOISE_FILTERING, ISOLATION, POWER_FILTERING, TRANSFORMING };
    1924                 :             : 
    1925                 :             :     /**
    1926                 :             :      * Type of the terminal

    1931                 :             : 
    1932                 :             :     /**
    1933                 :             :      * Topology that will use the magnetic

    1936                 :             :

    3101                 :             :     /**

    3120                 :             : 
    3121                 :             :     /**
    3122                 :             :      * List of rectangular winding windows
    3123                 :             :      *
    3124                 :             :      * It is the area between the winding column and the closest lateral column, and it
    3125                 :             :      * represents the area where all the wires of the magnetic will have to fit, and
    3126                 :             :      * equivalently, where all the current must circulate once, in the case of inductors, or
    3127                 :             :      * twice, in the case of transformers
    3128                 :             :      *
    3129                 :             :      * List of radial winding windows
    3130                 :             :      *
    3131                 :             :      * It is the area between the delimited between a height from the surface of the toroidal
    3132                 :             :      * core at a given angle, and it represents the area where all the wires of the magnetic
    3133                 :             :      * will have to fit, and equivalently, where all the current must circulate once, in the
    3134                 :             :      * case of inductors, or twice, in the case of transformers
    3135                 :             :      */

    3137                 :             :         public:

    3158                 :             :

    3162                 :             :          * join another half-set

    3291                 :             :     /**
    3292                 :             :      * The description of a bobbin
    3293                 :             :      */

    5308                 :             :     /**
    5309                 :             :      * A material for the magnetic cores
    5310                 :             :      */
    5311                 :             :     class CoreMaterial {
    5312                 :             :         public:

    5314                 :           1 :         virtual ~CoreMaterial() = default;
    5315                 :             :

    5358                 :             :

    5364                 :             :

    5819                 :             :         double minimum_area;
    5820                 :             : 
    5821                 :             :         public:
    5822                 :             :         /**

    5824                 :             :          * core is not uniform and its section changes along the path
    5825                 :             :          */
    5826                 :           0 :         const double & get_effective_area() const { return effective_area; }

    5829                 :             : 
    5830                 :             :         /**

    5833                 :           0 :         const double & get_effective_length() const { return effective_length; }

    5836                 :             : 
    5837                 :             :         /**

    5844                 :             : 
    5845                 :             :         /**

    5848                 :           0 :         const double & get_minimum_area() const { return minimum_area; }
    5849                 :             :         double & get_mutable_minimum_area() { return minimum_area; }
    5850                 :           0 :         void set_minimum_area(const double & value) { this->minimum_area = value; }
    5851                 :             :     };

    6977                 :             :     /**

    6979                 :             :      *
